what_it_does: Renders a design-system preview card showing four warm near-black ink tiers with names, hex values, sample text, and brief usage descriptions.
observations: Includes a @dsCard metadata comment identifying the card as Brand / Ink scale with a 700x200 viewport.
Links to _card.css and adds file-local CSS for card spacing, grid layout, swatches, chips, labels, and descriptions.
Defines four displayed ink roles: primary, secondary, muted, and inverse.
Shows primary, secondary, and muted as text samples on white, and inverse as light text on a dark ink surface.
Uses CSS custom properties for canvas, foreground colors, fonts, and border radius while hard-coding swatch hex values inline.
standouts: The lead copy states the ink palette is warm near-black and never pure black.
Each tier includes both a hex value and intended usage.
The inverse tier is demonstrated on a dark surface rather than on white.
conventions: Uses a compact self-contained HTML preview card structure.
Uses CSS variables for shared design-system primitives.
Uses inline styles for the color chips to keep the displayed swatch values close to the markup.
Uses short lowercase token names for color roles.
concerns: Inline color styles and visible hex labels must stay manually synchronized.
The file has no document language attribute.
The four-column grid has no file-local responsive fallback.
notes: This file is a compact visual ledger entry for the design system's warm ink text-color scale.
questions: Are these hex values source tokens or only preview examples?
Should inverse be considered part of the ink scale or a paired light-on-dark foreground token?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it presents four tiers of warm near-black ink with usage notes.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make ink color roles inspectable at a glance by pairing each role with a text sample, token-like name, hex value, and use case.
novelty: The explicit framing of ink as warm near-black and never pure black is a distinct design constraint in the file.
contribution: Contributes a warm ink foreground scale with semantic roles, exact hex values, sample rendering, and usage guidance for text hierarchy and inverse text on dark surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML design-system preview card documenting four warm ink foreground color tiers: primary #1F1A12, secondary #6B5F47, muted #A89678, and inverse #FBF7EC.
intention_for_embedding: It was for visually briefing and documenting the intended roles of a warm near-black ink palette in a design system.
